attractions.name: Eiffel Tower
attractions.description: Paris's iron-lattice icon, built for the 1889 World's Fair, with observation decks up to the top offering sweeping city views.
attractions.budget_category: Mid-range to high (higher levels cost more)
attractions.best_time: Early morning or after sunset for lights

attractions.name: Louvre Museum
attractions.description: The world's most-visited museum, home to the Mona Lisa, the Venus de Milo, and an immense collection spanning ancient to 19th-century art.
attractions.best_time: Weekday morning, book timed entry online

attractions.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
attractions.description: The Gothic masterpiece on Île de la Cité, reopened to visitors in December 2024 after a multi-year restoration following the 2019 fire.
attractions.budget_category: Free entry to the cathedral (towers/crypt extra)

attractions.name: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
attractions.description: A hilltop hilltop village of cobbled streets, artist squares (Place du Tertre), and the white-domed Sacré-Cœur Basilica with panoramic city views.
attractions.transport_tips: Anvers Metro station + funicular, or Abbesses station

attractions.name: Musée d'Orsay
attractions.description: A former railway station turned museum, holding the world's largest collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces.

attractions.name: Champs-Élysées & Arc de Triomphe
attractions.description: The grand boulevard running from Place de la Concorde to the Arc de Triomphe, lined with flagship boutiques, cafes, and cinemas.

attractions.name: Seine River Cruise
attractions.description: A relaxed boat cruise past Notre-Dame, the Louvre, and the Eiffel Tower — a classic way to see the city's landmarks from the water.
attractions.transport_tips: Departure points near Pont Neuf and Eiffel Tower

attractions.name: Palace of Versailles
attractions.description: The opulent former royal residence outside Paris, famous for the Hall of Mirrors and vast landscaped gardens — a popular full-day trip.
attractions.transport_tips: RER C train to Versailles Château–Rive Gauche

attractions.name: Luxembourg Gardens
attractions.description: Elegant formal gardens in the Left Bank surrounding the Luxembourg Palace, popular for picnics, toy sailboats on the pond, and people-watching.

restaurants.name: Le Comptoir du Relais, Saint-Germain
restaurants.description: A celebrated bistro known for classic French dishes and a lively neighbourhood atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Marché des Enfants Rouges
restaurants.description: Paris's oldest covered market (in Le Marais) with stalls serving everything from couscous to Japanese bento in a casual food-hall setting.

restaurants.name: Angelina, Rue de Rivoli
restaurants.description: A historic tearoom famous for its ultra-thick hot chocolate and Mont Blanc chestnut pastry.

hidden_gems.name: Coulée Verte René-Dumont
hidden_gems.description: An elevated park built on a disused railway viaduct — the inspiration for New York's High Line — offering a quiet, green walk above the 12th arrondissement's streets.

hidden_gems.name: Rue Crémieux
hidden_gems.description: A small, colourful pastel-painted residential street near Gare de Lyon, popular for photos but still relatively under the radar.

nightlife.name: Le Marais Bars
nightlife.description: A historic district with a dense concentration of bars, wine cellars, and LGBTQ+-friendly nightlife around Rue Vieille du Temple.

nightlife.name: Moulin Rouge Cabaret
nightlife.description: The legendary Montmartre cabaret known for its can-can revue — book well ahead for dinner-and-show packages.

shopping.name: Le Marais Boutiques
shopping.description: Independent designer boutiques, vintage shops, and concept stores lining the narrow streets of Le Marais.

festivals.name: Fête de la Musique
festivals.description: A city-wide free music festival with impromptu and organised performances on streets and squares across Paris.

festivals.name: Bastille Day
festivals.description: France's national day, marked by a military parade on the Champs-Élysées and fireworks at the Eiffel Tower.

adventure_activities.name: Catacombs of Paris
adventure_activities.description: An underground ossuary beneath the city streets lined with the bones of millions of Parisians — an atmospheric, slightly eerie must for the curious.

local_tips: Get a Navigo Easy or paper carnet of Metro tickets — the Metro is the fastest way around and runs late into the night.
Many museums are free or discounted on first Sundays of the month, and closed on Mondays or Tuesdays (varies by museum) — check before planning.
A polite 'Bonjour' when entering any shop or cafe goes a long way with Parisian service.
Book major sights (Eiffel Tower, Versailles, Louvre) online in advance — queues without a timed ticket can run hours in peak season.

safety.general: Paris is generally safe for tourists, but pickpocketing is a real risk in crowded tourist spots (Metro, Eiffel Tower, Montmartre) — keep bags zipped and in front of you.
safety.scams: Be wary of petition scams, friendship-bracelet scams near Sacré-Cœur, and unofficial 'gold ring' street scams.
safety.transport_safety: Some Metro lines/stations feel less comfortable late at night — stick to well-lit, busier routes after dark.

culture.overview: Paris takes pride in its art, gastronomy, and café culture; meals are unhurried and dining is treated as an experience, not just refuelling.
culture.etiquette: Greet with 'Bonjour/Bonsoir' before asking anything; tipping isn't mandatory (service is included) but rounding up is appreciated.

transport.getting_around: The Metro, RER, and buses cover the whole city extensively; walking is also very pleasant given how compact central Paris is.
transport.airport: Charles de Gaulle (CDG) and Orly (ORY) airports; RER B connects CDG to central Paris.

visa_information.indian_passport_holders: Indian citizens need a Schengen visa to visit France/Paris — apply well in advance with proof of accommodation and return travel.
visa_information.general: EU/EEA citizens and several other nationalities can enter visa-free for short stays under Schengen rules.

emergency_contacts.tourist_helpline: Paris Info via en.parisinfo.com for visitor assistance

weather.best_time_to_visit: April-June and September-October (mild weather, fewer crowds than peak summer)
weather.avoid: August (many local businesses close as Parisians go on holiday) if you want a fully 'local' experience
